note: Curated, user-facing release notes for the prod (main + demo) track. Keyed by the CH12 prod version. Add a new entry at the top of `releases` each time the prod version bumps (see chore(release) boundaries on main). Keep it readable — this is for users, not a git log.

releases.title: Faster, more accessible, more solid under the hood
releases.summary: A foundation pass: the app now loads as modern, self-contained modules — nothing changes in how it looks or works — alongside a batch of accessibility, performance, and reliability fixes.
highlights: Accessibility: the performance graph now reads each day's values aloud to screen readers as you arrow across it.
Performance: the performance graph stays snappy even with lots of day-notes saved.
Reliability: steadier trade imports and backup restores, and the data, export, and stat-detail dialogs now always release the page scroll when you close them.

releases.title: Tap a stat for the full story
releases.summary: The five headline stat cards on your dashboard are now clickable — open any of them for a focused breakdown with its own mini-chart. Promoted from the staging sandbox to the main app and the demo.
highlights: Click or tab+Enter on Net PnL, Win Rate, Profit Factor, Avg Win/Loss, or Max Drawdown to open a detail modal.
Each modal shows a metric-specific breakdown plus a focused inline chart (equity curve with peak→trough marks, day-of-week and per-symbol breakdowns, PnL distribution, and more).
Read-only — it's a deeper look at numbers you already have; nothing to configure or save.

releases.title: Filters refine the whole dashboard
releases.summary: Filtering once again narrows your entire dashboard — the calendar, stat cards, cost budget, statistics, and the performance graph all respond together, the way they always have.

releases.title: The full dashboard, everywhere
releases.summary: Every feature that was being trialed in the staging sandbox is now live on the main app and the demo — the journal you use day-to-day gets the whole redesigned dashboard.
highlights: New edge-to-edge web-grid dashboard on wide screens, with drag-to-reorder modules and a full-width performance graph.
Activity terminal, a session-status pill, and saveable workspace layouts.
Saved filter views, an in-app export-report modal (PDF / PNG / JPEG / Markdown + email), collapsible per-symbol cost & tax breakdowns, and day-note dots on the equity curve.

releases.title: Demo matches the app
releases.summary: The demo is now a faithful 1:1 mirror of the real app: every panel and control is present, just with data-changing actions disabled on the sample data.
highlights: Restored the Changelog link and the Day-notes section in the demo.
Data-mutating controls (load, import, backup, edit, delete) are clearly disabled rather than missing.

releases.title: Versioned release notes
releases.summary: This changelog is new. Instead of a raw list of code changes, it's now curated release notes — what actually shipped in each version, written to be read. You're looking at the first one.
highlights: Every release gets a plain-language summary and highlights, newest first.
Tracks the live production app and demo.

releases.title: Stability & security pass
releases.summary: A sweep of fixes from an internal audit — tightening up the calendar, your data, and the behind-the-scenes release machinery.
highlights: Clicking inside the data-manager or export popup no longer clears the calendar day you had selected.
Your journal note is saved the instant you switch days, closing a rare window where a quick edit could be dropped.
Imported backup screenshots are now validated before they're shown, and release versioning is hardened against edge cases.

releases.title: Admin & launch reliability
releases.summary: A maintenance release fixing the internal admin tools.
highlights: The admin version panel no longer hangs on load, and launching the staging environment is more reliable.

releases.title: Calendar notes & cost polish
releases.summary: Quality-of-life improvements to day-by-day journaling and the cost estimates that power your P&L.
highlights: Smoother editing of per-day notes straight from the calendar.
Refinements to commission, fee, and terminal-cost calculations.

releases.title: Automated release versioning
releases.summary: Every release now carries a real version number, derived automatically when changes ship — so this changelog and the in-app badge always agree on what you're running.
highlights: Version badge in the app header reflects the live release.
Separate prod and staging tracks behind the scenes.

releases.title: Beta released
releases.summary: The first public Beta of Blotterbook — a fast, private, browser-based futures-trading journal. Everything before automated versioning is rolled up here: the dashboard and equity curve, the demo, the marketing site, broker/platform format auto-detection, a CSV importer, an IndexedDB-backed data manager, filters, the day journal, and report export.
